title: Bump to 1.33.2
body:
I'm doing some mad git hackery unlike anything I've done before.  I'm attempting to merge this commit between these two existing commits:

commit [12b205f69876bf09bb6dfe03adcdefad5d4ee9f8](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/12b205f69876bf09bb6dfe03adcdefad5d4ee9f8)
Remove makefile targets entrypoint{,.gz} for non x86_64

and 

commit [c36ab60ca472e182e3dcd0d51df3f383b108a665](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/c36ab60ca472e182e3dcd0d51df3f383b108a665)
 Merge pull request #5007 from containers/renovate/github.com-fsouza-go-dockerclient-1.x

That way, we will get just the changes added after 1.33.1 that deal with removing bloat and won't get the non-versioned c/{project} version bumps too.  I can then in theory make v1.33.2 with only the bloat fixes.

:crossed_fingers: 

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
@vrothberg if you could eyeball the hackery, I'd appreciate it.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I think it will be more easier if we create `release-1.33` branch from https://github.com/containers/buildah/releases/tag/v1.33.1 and then backport needed commits there and cut `release-1.33.2` from that branch. Wouldn't it ? And we can bump `main` to `1.34` or something. 

But I'm not sure when do we cut branches so please feel free to ignore comment if it is not applicable.
+1 to @flouthoc comment, this should really be done by using a branch and backporting what you need. If we need to to another release this approach here would no longer work.

> git rebase bump-1.33.2 main

(I don’t know that this did anything… I read that as updating the `main` branch on top of `bump-1.33.2`; that wouldn’t show up in this PR.)

---

If I understand this correctly, the goal is to create something like this?

```
  /-.-.-.-.-\
-.           .-main
  \-.-x-----/
```
where `x` is the tagged release?

Yes, that would work (and it probably _is_ extensible to doing it again and tagging more 1.33.x releases in a similar way), but AFAICS creating a branch and not merging it into `main` would be simpler in all respects.

E.g. if we have a branch, we can _choose_ to run dedicated CI to ensure that branch continues to build.

(There is an argument for keeping release branches _and always merging them into `main`, per https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20180323-01/?p=98325, but we don’t currently work that way and I am not proposing we start. And that would still work better with a named branch.)
